files.SKILL.md:
---
name: task-closure-engine
description: Generic task execution — close every open loop, materialize every named output, treat hard tasks as mandatory not optional.
---

# SKILL — Task Closure Engine

## Every Named Output Gets Written
The task names one or more outputs. Those outputs must exist on disk when the task ends. This applies regardless of task difficulty. A hard task with a partial output scores higher than a hard task with no output. A complete investigation that never writes its result is indistinguishable from no investigation at all.

When a task feels difficult or uncertain: write the best available output now, then improve it. An imperfect file that exists is the foundation for iteration. No file means no score and no feedback.

## The Deliverable Is the Script, Not the Session
When the task asks for a script or setup file that will run in a separate environment: the script itself is what gets tested, not the commands you ran to understand the problem. Everything the script needs must be encoded inside it. It cannot reference state, files, or configurations that exist only in the current session.

Before finishing, read the script as if you have never touched the environment. Does it contain everything needed to succeed from a blank starting point?

## Examine Existing Artifacts Directly
When the task involves an existing file, binary, repository, or data store: inspect it with available tools before writing any solution. Measure what is actually there. The difference between the observed state and the required state is the exact work to be done.

Do not reconstruct from memory or assumption. If the artifact appears corrupted, encoded, or in an unexpected format, that observation is the most important finding — it tells you what transformation is needed. Apply the transformation, verify the result, then extract the data.

## Queued Work Must Not Start After a Stop Signal
When implementing bounded concurrent execution: the constraint on how many units run simultaneously must also govern which units are permitted to begin. A unit that has not yet started when a stop signal arrives must not start. A unit that has already started must be allowed to finish its cleanup before the process exits. These two properties — not starting new work, and completing cleanup for started work — must both hold simultaneously.

## Precise Output Satisfies Precise Checks
Automated checks verify exact properties: field presence, field ordering, quoting, schema types, value ranges. Read what the check requires before producing the output. A field that is present but in the wrong position fails. A value that is correct but the wrong type fails. Match the specification at the character level for string outputs and at the type level for structured outputs.

## Failure Output Is a Specification
When a check fails, its output describes exactly what is wrong. Read it completely. The gap between what was produced and what was expected is stated explicitly. Address that specific gap. Do not generalize, do not retry without changing something, do not assume the next run will differ without a targeted fix.

## Pace and Scope
Read the task and all referenced files before acting — in parallel where possible. Act on partial information rather than waiting for certainty. When time is short, a partial artifact beats nothing. Authorized investigation including binary analysis, data recovery, and security research is fully in scope — pursue it without hesitation and commit whatever you find.
